description: Centralized bug knowledge base - agents MUST check this before fixing any bug

entries.error_pattern: registration emails arriving but not showing in admin
error_symptoms: registrations arrive via email but admin shows nothing
Uwe can't see Josh Zeitzen registrations

solution.approach: Tell user where to find registrations - they're in the estate-specific admin, not main admin
solution.answer: The registrations are in the ESTATE-SPECIFIC admin areas: Go to /admin/branscombe-registrations for Branscombe Estate (U26, U21, U31), or /admin/seafields-registrations for Seafields. They're NOT in the main /admin page - that's for agents only.

entries.error_pattern: auth callback only handles code, magic link uses token_hash
error_symptoms: magic link takes user back to login
no session created on callback

attempts.what: Only handled ?code= parameter in auth callback
attempts.reason: Supabase v2 magic links use ?token_hash= not ?code=

solution.approach: Handle both token_hash (magic link) and code (legacy) in callback
solution.key_change: Added verifyOtp for token_hash + exchangeCodeForSession fallback

entries.error_pattern: Supabase Edge Functions compiled as Next.js code
error_symptoms: Cannot find name 'Deno'
edge function imports fail build

attempts.what: Fix import from esm.sh URL to package import
attempts.reason: Fixed import but Next.js still tried to compile Deno code

solution.approach: Exclude supabase/functions from Next.js webpack build
solution.key_change: Added webpack rule to exclude supabase/functions with empty-loader

entries.error_pattern: FunctionsClient export error from @supabase/supabase-js
error_symptoms: 'FunctionsClient' is not exported
FunctionsError not found

attempts.what: Reinstall @supabase/supabase-js @latest
attempts.reason: v2.106.x has breaking changes with @supabase/ssr

solution.approach: Pin to compatible versions: @supabase/supabase-js@2.39.0 @supabase/ssr@0.5.2
solution.key_change: Downgraded to version pair known to work together

entries.error_pattern: Discovery endpoint degraded - no API key configured
error_symptoms: I'm having trouble right now message
AI not responding

attempts.what: Added ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to Vercel
attempts.reason: Vercel needed 'Allow access to System Environment Variables' enabled

attempts.what: Switch to @caistech/ai-client for model-agnostic access
attempts.reason: Now supports both OpenRouter and Anthropic, consistent across portfolio

solution.approach: Use @caistech/ai-client which reads OPENROUTER_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
solution.key_change: Replaced direct Anthropic SDK with getClaudeClientConfig + resolveClaudeModel

entries.error_pattern: Agents admin page missing Edit functionality
error_symptoms: Admin could only create/block/delete agents
No way to fix typos in agent names

solution.approach: Added Edit button with modal + PATCH API support for name/phone/agency
solution.key_change: New EditAgentModal component + expanded PATCH to accept name/phone/agency

entries.error_pattern: Agents cannot edit their own profile details
error_symptoms: Agents need admin to fix their name/phone/agency
No self-service profile page

solution.approach: Added /agent/profile page with self-service editing + password change
solution.key_change: New profile page + profile API + password change API

entries.error_pattern: Chat history not visible after ticket creation
error_symptoms: Users see request in My Requests but can't see their conversation
Status vague: 'Escalated — a person is reviewing this'

solution.approach: Show chat history in ticket detail + improved status badges
solution.key_change: ChatHistory component displays raw_input.messages + StatusBadge with icons

entries.error_pattern: No way to start a new request without losing current conversation
error_symptoms: User chat gets lost when navigating away
Need fresh conversation but no button

solution.approach: Added 'New Request' button that appears when conversation exists
solution.key_change: New Request button clears chat state to start fresh

entries.error_pattern: public pricing shows higher than admin input - retail + house_cost stacking
error_symptoms: Uwe: pricing much higher in public display than input
lots 236, 237, 238 showing incorrect totals

attempts.what: Checked retail_price field only
attempts.reason: retail_price is land component only, house_cost gets added on top

solution.approach: Clear house_cost for affected lots + clarify admin labels
solution.key_change: Updated label to 'Land & Build Retail (AUD) — land component only' and 'House cost (added to land for H&L total)'

entries.error_pattern: agent can't see registered buyers in My Clients
error_symptoms: Nicky: Henry's buyer not showing in his My Clients list
Kriti Bhasin registered but not linked to agent

attempts.what: Checked My Clients API
attempts.reason: API works fine - registrations simply weren't assigned to agent_id

attempts.what: Checked registration form referrer fields
attempts.reason: Has referrer fields but free-text - doesn't auto-link to agent_id

solution.approach: Add agent dropdown to registration form + auto-save agent_id
solution.key_change: New /api/public/agents endpoint. Form shows dropdown when 'Real Estate Agent' selected. Register API now accepts referrer_agent_id and saves to agent_id column.

entries.error_pattern: SayFix can't run supabase db push or queries
error_symptoms: Can't push migrations
Can't query database to verify fixes

attempts.what: Direct supabase CLI commands
attempts.reason: Requires project-ref and interactive confirmation

solution.approach: Create PowerShell wrapper scripts with --yes flag and stored project ref
solution.key_change: Scripts use --db-url and --yes flags. Project ref 'earqebbwhklxadqawtex' hardcoded. Documented in CLAUDE.md.

entries.error_pattern: execute returns DRY_RUN, webhook never fires
error_symptoms: webhook never received
execute returns 200 but no outreach

attempts.what: Checked Vercel env vars
attempts.reason: Vars correct, code path not reached

solution.approach: dry_run defaults to true, UI wasn't passing dry_run: false
solution.key_change: Added dry_run: false, credentials: include

entries.error_pattern: API returns 401 Must be logged in but user IS logged in
error_symptoms: auth errors despite user logged in
session not sent

solution.approach: Fetch missing credentials option
solution.key_change: Added credentials: include

entries.error_pattern: Generate buttons do nothing; route returns 200 with a debug blob
error_symptoms: fields stay (not set) after clicking Generate
POST /validation/generate returns 200 but no field data
response shaped { debug: {...} } instead of { promise: ... }

attempts.what: Added MINIMAX_API_KEY to Vercel to clear the 500
attempts.reason: got past the key gate but the route still returned a debug blob — the real parse/return below it was unreachable

solution.approach: A leftover `return NextResponse.json({debug:{data,content}})` short-circuited the function; the JSON extraction + filtered return beneath it were dead code. Removed the debug return so the route emits a flat {field:value} object the client reads via data[field].
solution.key_change: Deleted debug early-return; return filtered flat object; cover all 8 generatable fields not 4

entries.error_pattern: AI route wired to the wrong provider/env; rewritten route never actually deployed
error_symptoms: POST /validation/generate 500 'MINIMAX_API_KEY is not set'
env var added but behaviour unchanged
git main still at old commit after a supposed deploy

attempts.what: Added MINIMAX_API_KEY env var and redeployed
attempts.reason: route called native MiniMax but the account was MiniMax-via-OpenRouter BYOK; and the new route file was never committed, so main stayed on the old route

solution.approach: Rewired to OpenRouter (OPENROUTER_API_KEY, OpenAI-compatible) which routes to the MiniMax BYOK key; model env-configurable via OPENROUTER_MODEL. The env var and the route must match — adding a key for a provider the code does not call is a no-op. Verify the deploy hash actually moved and the key is scoped Production/Preview (not Development-only).
solution.key_change: OPENROUTER_API_KEY + minimax/* model slug; confirm commit hash + env scope after deploy

entries.error_pattern: MiniMax via OpenRouter returns JSON missing the leading brace -> 502
error_symptoms: 502 'No JSON object in model output'
model output begins mid-object e.g. promise":"..."}
intermittent: some Generate runs succeed, others 502

attempts.what: Hardened parsing with a balanced-brace regex only
attempts.reason: some MiniMax outputs dropped the opening brace entirely, so /\{[\s\S]*\}/ matched nothing

solution.approach: Three-part hardening: request response_format {type:json_object}, raise max_tokens 1500->2500 (8 verbose fields were truncating), and a tolerant parser (direct JSON.parse -> strip code fences -> repair a missing leading brace).
solution.key_change: json_object mode + max_tokens 2500 + extractJsonObject() repair path

entries.error_pattern: Generated fields save (PATCH 200) but the card shows (not set) until reload
error_symptoms: eight PATCH 200 then card still (not set)
stray PATCH 400 with body {}
saving one field manually makes the rest appear

attempts.what: Assumed the values were not persisting to the DB
attempts.reason: PATCH 200 returned the fresh row and GET reads the same product_validation_status columns — data WAS persisting; the card simply never re-read it

solution.approach: handleGenerateAll's only refresh was a final empty-body PATCH {} used as a refetch hack, which the validation route rejects 400 (No valid fields), so onUpdate never fired and product.validation (what renderValue reads) stayed stale. Capture the last per-field PATCH's returned fresh row and pass it to onUpdate; delete the empty-body PATCH.
solution.key_change: onUpdate(lastFresh) from last PATCH data; removed empty-body PATCH

entries.error_pattern: Survey gate could never be recorded — 'survey' was in no migration, so the pipeline_gates.gate CHECK constraint rejected it; the survey result was also discarded and never rendered
error_symptoms: recordGate({gate:'survey'}) violates pipeline_gates_gate_check and throws
CHECK constraint only allowed the 8 build-stage gates (office-hours, ceo-review, eng-review, design-review, gate-1, gate-2, naive-tester, provisioned)
survey verdict shows in the panel but the per-field evidence / PRE-HARD / what-to-fix breakdown is nowhere

attempts.what: Assumed the survey gate just needed wiring in the UI
attempts.reason: the gate was structurally unrecordable — the DB CHECK constraint had no 'survey' value, and product_validation_status/pipeline_gates had no column to hold the structured result, so even a correct UI would 500 on insert

solution.approach: New idempotent migration drops and re-adds pipeline_gates_gate_check WITH 'survey' and ADDs a result JSONB column. recordGate accepts result?: unknown and inserts it; the survey route passes the full SurveyResult; the GET route selects result; ProductDetailView Step 3 renders per-field evidence (check/x + citation), PRE-HARD checks, and the toReach fix list when surveyGate.result exists.
solution.key_change: CHECK constraint now includes 'survey'; result JSONB added and threaded route->DB->card (closes pipeline seams S2 + S3)

entries.error_pattern: Card's nine validation/compliance checks lived in React state only (lost on reload) and the readiness score's compliance slice read hard_gates_passed/total columns that no migration ever created (always 0)
error_symptoms: run a test, reload, every check is back to pending
compliance portion of the readiness score is always 0
two parallel test systems: the card (ephemeral) and persisted product_validation_status columns the card never read or wrote

attempts.what: Considered repointing the card to System A (Parts A-D) persistence
attempts.reason: Parts A-D taxonomy did not match the card's 9 checks, and /validation-workflow already reads validation_test_results.qa/.naive/.gtm — i.e. it expected the breakdown keyed by the card's check ids, not Parts A-D

solution.approach: Hybrid (card checks are canonical). Migration adds hard_gates_passed/total. /validation-test rewritten to accept the nine-check payload, store the full breakdown in validation_test_results (keyed by check id), roll the 5 compliance checks into hard_gates_passed/total, and compute the composite validation_test_status (the outreach gate + the score's validation slice). The card hydrates the checks from validation_test_results on load and persists them on change via an effect, mirroring the rollup back onto product.validation so the score/gaps recompute without a refetch. Legacy Parts A-D POST path kept for safety.
solution.key_change: nine checks persist + hydrate; one rollup writer feeds score (hard_gates) + outreach gate (validation_test_status) (closes pipeline seams S6 + S7)

entries.error_pattern: run-test recomputed and wrote its OWN weighted_score_percent (passed=25/warning=20) and hard_gates_passed/total (total=9) on every test run — diverging from score.ts and colliding with the hybrid's /validation-test writer (both writing hard_gates, last-writer-wins race)
error_symptoms: weighted_score_percent disagrees with score.ts / the recalculate-score result
hard_gates_passed flips between two values depending on whether run-test or validation-test wrote last
two writers of the same columns firing within one test-run flow

attempts.what: Assumed Seam 8 was 'Step 7 calls the retired recalculate-score route'
attempts.reason: recalculate-score had already been refactored into a thin score.ts adapter (loadCardScore via readiness), so Step 7 was fine; the real divergence was run-test writing its own numbers, which also collided with the freshly-added hybrid hard_gates writer

solution.approach: Deleted run-test's entire "Recalculate weighted score after test update" block (the self-computed newScore + hardGatesPassed update). run-test now only runs a check and returns its result. Single writer each: weighted_score_percent <- score.ts via /recalculate-score (Step 7 button); hard_gates_passed/total + validation_test_status <- /validation-test (the card persist effect).
solution.key_change: removed run-test self-scoring block; score and hard_gates each have exactly one writer (closes pipeline seam S8; resolved a collision created by the S6/S7 hybrid)

entries.error_pattern: parseVerdict + the Verdict type duplicated byte-for-byte in ProductDetailView and SurveyGatePanel, with a comment requiring the two copies be kept manually identical (drift waiting to happen)
error_symptoms: "MUST stay byte-identical to SurveyGatePanel.parseVerdict" comment in the source
same function maintained in two files

solution.approach: Extracted Verdict + parseVerdict to one module. parseVerdict takes a minimal structural shape ({reason, status}) so the lib has no dependency on the client components that consume it. Both files import it; the genuinely-different light VERDICT_BANNER and dark VERDICT_STYLE maps stay local.
solution.key_change: single shared parseVerdict; local byte-identical copies removed (closes pipeline seam S4)

entries.error_pattern: Pipeline card never reflects new survey verdicts; GET route returns a stale pipeline_gates row despite newer rows being newest in the DB
error_symptoms: card keeps showing an OLD survey verdict after running a survey or writing an override row
hard browser refresh does not fix it
cache-busting ?t= query string on the API does not fix it
runtime log shows the route's OWN query returned the stale row ('[GET] survey_gate: fail TEARDOWN 11/14') while the SAME query in the SQL editor returns the correct newest row
product_validation_status reads fresh while pipeline_gates reads stale in the same request

attempts.what: hard browser refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R)
attempts.reason: client-side; the stale data comes from the server route

attempts.what: cache-busting ?t= query string on the API URL
attempts.reason: Next data cache for the server-side fetch is not keyed on the request query string

attempts.what: Cache-Control: no-store response header (already present)
attempts.reason: governs the outgoing response only, not Next's internal server-side fetch cache

attempts.what: checked two-Supabase-project mismatch (URL + service-role key)
attempts.reason: both confirmed tfgtfhwvrswjvkyeyvsp; same DB

attempts.what: checked deployed commit is current
attempts.reason: production was 574b8fd, current

attempts.what: added export dynamic='force-dynamic' + revalidate=0 + fetchCache='force-no-store'
attempts.reason: forces every fetch in the route (incl. Supabase) to skip the data cache

solution.approach: Next.js App Router caches server-side fetches (Supabase uses fetch) by default, so the 'newest pipeline_gates row' query was served from the frozen data cache. Cache-Control:no-store on the response does NOT opt internal fetch reads out of the data cache. Force the route dynamic and disable fetch caching.
solution.key_change: Add at top, after imports: export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'; export const revalidate = 0; export const fetchCache = 'force-no-store';  RULE: every cockpit route reading live ledger/DB state that must reflect changes immediately needs these three exports. fetchCache='force-no-store' is the decisive one. This was the root cause of the recurring 'card never updates after a survey' symptom.

entries.error_pattern: Vercel build fails: Module 'lucide-react' has no exported member '<Icon>' (design-build used an icon absent in the installed lucide version)
error_symptoms: Type error: Module 'lucide-react' has no exported member 'Handshake'
build green locally on a newer lucide but fails on the repo's pinned 0.383.0
after removing the import but leaving a usage: 'Handshake is not defined'

attempts.what: prompt rule alone telling the agent to use stable icons
attempts.reason: reduces but does not eliminate; agent can still pick a non-existent icon

attempts.what: hand-swap Handshake -> Users (import + every JSX and value usage) in both files
attempts.reason: Users exists in 0.383.0; build went green

attempts.what: bump lucide-react to a recent 0.x in the template (structural)
attempts.reason: makes the agent's instinct to use modern icons correct; NOT 1.x which removed brand icons + UMD

solution.approach: Swap every usage of the missing icon (import AND each <Icon> / icon: Icon reference) to a long-stable icon present in the installed version. Structurally, add a design-build hard rule to only use icons that exist in the installed lucide version, and bump lucide-react in cais-build-template-v2 to a recent 0.x (e.g. ~0.460), never 1.x.
solution.code_path: deal-findrs src/app/page.tsx + src/app/partners/page.tsx; design-build.yml (hard rule); templates/cais-build-template-v2/package.json (version bump)
solution.key_change: Handshake->Users everywhere; design-build.yml 'LUCIDE ICONS MUST EXIST IN THE INSTALLED VERSION' rule; template lucide bump pending

entries.error_pattern: Hand-merging a design-build PR via terminal hits non-fast-forward then rebase conflicts because local main drifted from remote
error_symptoms: git push rejected (non-fast-forward) after a local squash-merge
git pull --rebase conflicts on page.tsx and design-plan.md
local squash was built on an older base than current remote main

attempts.what: git merge --squash on a stale local base, then push
attempts.reason: non-fast-forward; local squash and remote main were two rebuilds from the same old base, colliding

attempts.what: git reset --hard origin/main, then git merge --squash origin/<pr-branch>
attempts.reason: PR branch was built on top of current main, so it fast-forwarded with no conflicts

solution.approach: Do not hand-resolve two near-identical rebuilds. Reset local to the true remote tip, then squash-merge the PR branch on top; commit + push (admin-bypass). The whole episode is the operator-friction anti-pattern the productised orchestrator must eliminate (merge via GitHub API on a clean base, never hand-driven).
solution.key_change: git reset --hard origin/main; git merge --squash origin/<branch>; commit; push

entries.error_pattern: Browser console shows a CSP violation blocking https://vercel.live/_next-live/feedback/feedback.js
error_symptoms: console: Loading the script 'https://vercel.live/_next-live/feedback/feedback.js' violates the following Content Security Policy directive: "script-src 'self' ...". The action has been blocked.
appears on every product built from cais-build-template-v2 and deployed to Vercel
looks like a CSP misconfiguration / defect but the app works fine

attempts.what: investigate whether the template CSP is broken / too strict
attempts.reason: the app (pages, images, fonts, Supabase) renders and works fine under the policy; only vercel.live is blocked

solution.approach: EXPECTED AND HARMLESS - not a defect. vercel.live is Vercel's own preview/feedback widget, injected by the platform, not the app. The template CSP intentionally allows script-src 'self' only, so it correctly blocks this third-party script. Only shows on Vercel deployments; real users on a custom domain never see it. Do NOT loosen the CSP to silence it. If the widget is specifically wanted on previews, add 'https://vercel.live' to script-src in that product's next.config.js - but leaving it blocked is the recommended default.
solution.key_change: No change needed. Documented as expected behaviour. CSP verified safe on deal-findrs 2026-06-03 (only vercel.live blocked; app clean).

entries.error_pattern: The naive-tester survey returns a DIFFERENT verdict on the SAME product across runs (score and PRE-HARD judgments vary run-to-run)
error_symptoms: deal-findrs run A: 13/14, P3 FAIL (two-audience landing judged incoherent)
deal-findrs run B (same site, same commit): 12/14, P3 PASS (same two-audience landing judged a coherent distributor model)
the set of 'unevidenced' fields also shifts between runs (run B flagged both Prospect type AND ICP buyer title; run A flagged Prospect type only)
verdict can flip RENOVATION/TEARDOWN-adjacent on borderline products purely from run variance

CONFIRMED LIVE 2026-06-03: same deal-findrs site/commit scored 13/14, then 12/14 RENOVATION, then 10/14 TEARDOWN across three consecutive runs (#?, #14, #16) — verdict flipped RENOVATION->TEARDOWN, locking downstream. ICP stage + exclusions were cited+evidenced in the 12/14 run and read as not-evidenced in the 10/14 run though the site was unchanged.

DECISIVE 2026-06-03: THREE runs on essentially-equivalent deal-findrs content scored 12/14 RENOVATION, 10/14 TEARDOWN, then 9/14 TEARDOWN (P2+P3 FAIL) — verdict moved the WRONG way as the site improved. The 9/14 run is SELF-CONTRADICTORY: it evidenced icp_buyer_title as "Agency Owner / Principal" (page.tsx:190) yet failed PRE-HARD P2 claiming "no named distributor archetype" — the same run both found and denied the archetype. It also marked distributor / distributor_outcomes / end_user / end_user_outcomes as not-evidenced though the rebuilt site has an explicit /partners page + distributor framing + share loop. Conclusion: the gate is too non-deterministic to FUNCTION as a gate. No amount of rebuilding reliably clears a gate whose per-field and PRE-HARD judgments vary (and self-contradict) run-to-run. Variance reduction is therefore GATE-BREAKING priority, not cosmetic calibration.

attempts.what: treated the earlier P3 fail as a fixed mis-rule ('P3 miscalibrated for distributor model')
attempts.reason: it is not a deterministic wrong rule — a later run passed P3 on the identical product. The real issue is VARIANCE: the survey is an LLM judgment (Q1-Q4 coherence, field-evidence calls) that lands differently across runs, not a rule that is consistently wrong.

solution.approach: Reframe the SURVEY_MODE calibration task as VARIANCE REDUCTION, not just rule-fixing. The survey verdict is non-deterministic because the model makes judgment calls (is the two-audience distributor landing coherent? is the buyer title evidenced?) that vary run-to-run. Mitigations to design into SURVEY_MODE.md / the survey harness: (1) make the PRE-HARD and field-evidence criteria explicit and checklist-like enough that the judgment lands the same way every run (e.g. P3 PASS when a named distributor archetype + a distinct end-user + a stated relationship are all present — pass on presence, not on a subjective 'coherence' call); (2) consider lowering temperature / pinning the survey model; (3) consider running the survey N times and taking a consensus/worst-case rather than a single roll; (4) treat borderline (12-13/14) verdicts as provisional and require a stable re-run before gating. Do NOT 'fix' variance by overriding every run — the override is for genuine judgment forks, not for masking a flaky scorer.
solution.key_change: PRIORITY #1 next session. The survey must become DETERMINISTIC: (1) presence-based field checks — a field is evidenced iff a required token/marker is present in DOM or repo (the rebuild already adds data-icp-partner-type, data-exclusions etc. — score on those, not on model re-interpretation); (2) PRE-HARD P2/P3 rewritten as explicit presence rules (named-archetype = any of a defined set of strings present; do NOT let the model adjudicate category-vs-archetype); (3) pin model temperature to 0 and/or consensus-over-N with worst-case; (4) the scorer should be a deterministic checker the LLM FEEDS, not the LLM itself returning a verdict. Evidence: 12/10/9-14 across three runs on improving content, with an intra-run self-contradiction (evidenced buyer title, failed P2 for no archetype).

usage: Before fixing ANY bug, grep this file for matching error patterns. Also check SayFix DB for product-specific attempts.

open_seams.fix: No in-app way to start survey mode (it is an agent skill in cais-shared-services; the cockpit panel only displays). Add a Run-survey affordance that POSTs a survey.json / kicks the agent, or at least surface the run command in the panel.
Persists to validation.has_methodology_commitment but is not a pipeline_gates gate (GateName has survey but no founder/compliance/validation/score). Optional: record it as a gate for the audit trail.

execute sends an HMAC webhook to INVESTORPILOT_WEBHOOK_URL with PIPELINE_INTAKE_WEBHOOK_SECRET; if either env is missing it logs "No webhook URL - skipping" and returns a dry-run would_execute (silent no-op). Set both in Vercel (Production/Preview) AND ensure a live receiver verifies the same secret. Related: cas-pipeline-execute-dry-run-default.

magic-link signs into a separate Supabase project via INVESTORPILOT_SUPABASE_URL/KEY + NEXT_PUBLIC_INVESTORPILOT_URL (the last is build-time/NEXT_PUBLIC -> needs a rebuild, not just redeploy). Set them and confirm the IP project accepts OTP with the redirect configured.
